testing_strategy: Unit: evaluatePolicy('abc', {minLen:10,classes:3,banlist:[]}) returns length + classes issues. k-anonymity: spy on fetch in breachCount; assert the request URL contains only the 5-char prefix and the raw password never appears in any request body (negative assertion). Fail-open: mock the range API to throw; assert PasswordField shows 'unknown' and the server route still creates the user. Server: POST a banlisted password; assert 422 and (side-effect) no user created. Chaos: breach API returns garbage; assert breachCount returns 0 (treated as not-found) and signup proceeds.
what_breaks_naive_approach: POSTing the full SHA-1 hash to a breach API discloses the user's exact password choice to a third party (the whole point of k-anonymity is to never do this), and a fail-closed breach check turns a HIBP outage into a total signup outage — both are privacy/availability regressions the prefix-only, fail-open design specifically prevents.

title: Convert a healthcare appointment form to schema-driven validation with async slot-hold to prevent double-booking
business_context: A telehealth scheduler lets patients pick a provider + time slot. Today the form validates fields independently and books on submit, but two patients can pick the same slot simultaneously and both think they're booked; the second gets a confusing error after entering insurance details. ~4% of bookings collide at peak. We want to hold the slot the instant it's selected and validate insurance async, releasing the hold if the patient abandons.
constraints: Selecting a slot must place a short-lived hold so a concurrent patient sees it as taken
Hold expires (TTL 8 min) if the patient doesn't complete; expiry must free the slot
Insurance eligibility check is async (p95 1.1s) and must run while the hold is active, not block selection
Booking confirmation must convert the hold atomically (hold->booked) or fail if hold expired
Works across multiple scheduler instances (distributed hold, not in-process)
Backward compat: providers' existing calendar sync must still see holds as tentative

before_state.description: Independent field validation; book-on-submit; concurrent patients double-book; late insurance failure.

issues: No hold on selection -> two patients pick same slot
Insurance failure surfaces only at submit
409 after the patient already filled everything
No distributed coordination across instances

reasoning: Naive approach: on submit, SELECT...FOR UPDATE the slot and insert if free. Rejected: it prevents the DB double-insert but the collision is only discovered at submit after the patient typed insurance — the bad UX we're paid to fix — and it doesn't reflect 'taken' to the other patient's live picker. Alternative: lock the slot in-process when picked; rejected because there are multiple scheduler instances, so an in-process lock is invisible to a patient hitting a different instance. Chosen path: selecting a slot calls a hold endpoint that SET NX EX in Redis with the patient's session (distributed, TTL 8m) so other pickers see it taken (async/data); insurance eligibility runs while the hold is active without blocking (async); confirm converts hold->booked atomically via a Lua script checking the hold still belongs to this session (data); a release on abandon/expiry frees the slot; calendar sync reads holds as tentative (config). Tests cover concurrent hold, expiry release, atomic confirm.

after_state.description: Distributed slot-hold on selection, async insurance during hold, atomic hold->booked confirm, TTL release, calendar-aware.

files.purpose: Distributed hold/confirm/release with atomic owner-checked conversion
Atomic confirm-then-persist; authoritative insurance; clear expiry message
Hold-on-select, async insurance during hold, release on abandon (beacon)

migration_strategy: Add Redis hold/confirm/release helpers and the /api/hold + release endpoints (no UI wiring yet)
Have calendar sync treat active holds as tentative blocks (additive)
Ship the hold-on-select Booking flow behind flag slot_holds off; book() now requires a valid hold
Canary a few providers; verify concurrent pickers see slots as taken and abandons release within TTL
Ramp to all; keep book()'s atomic confirm permanently as the double-book guard
Rollback: flag off restores book-on-submit (DB FOR UPDATE still prevents double-insert)
testing_strategy: Integration: two holdSlot calls for the same slot concurrently; assert exactly one returns true (side-effect: one Redis key, owned by the first session). Confirm: confirmHold by a non-owner session returns false and leaves the hold; by the owner returns true and deletes it. Expiry: set a 1s TTL in a test; after expiry assert another patient can hold the slot. Booking: book() when the hold expired returns 409 with the 'hold expired' message and creates no appointment. Chaos: Redis unavailable on confirm; assert book() fails closed (no booking) rather than double-booking, and surfaces a retry.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A submit-time FOR UPDATE prevents the duplicate DB row but discovers the collision only after the patient typed insurance, so the loser still hits the late 409 we set out to eliminate; and an in-process lock is invisible across the multiple scheduler instances, so two patients on different instances both see the slot as free and both proceed.

title: Add cross-field password-confirm and async email-taken validation to an Angular reactive signup form
business_context: An Angular reactive-forms signup checks fields independently. Password/confirm mismatch is only caught on submit, and email-taken is a 409 with a generic toast, so users complete the form before learning their email is taken. ~10% re-attempt. We want a cross-field confirm validator and an async email-taken validator wired into Angular's reactive forms, with the same uniqueness rule enforced server-side.
constraints: Password/confirm must validate as a cross-field group validator (not per-control)
Email-taken is an async validator (p95 200ms) debounced; pending state must disable submit
Async validator must cancel stale requests when the email changes again
Same uniqueness enforced server-side (async validator is advisory)
Form must not submit while async validation is pending
Backward compat: existing submit endpoint contract unchanged

before_state.description: Per-control validation; confirm checked at submit; email-taken is a late generic 409.

issues: Confirm mismatch only at submit via alert
No async email-taken validator -> late 409
No cancellation of stale async checks
Generic toast hides which field failed

reasoning: Naive approach: add a manual subscription on the email valueChanges that fires an HTTP call. Rejected: manual subscriptions don't integrate with Angular's validity state (submit can still fire while a check is in flight) and don't cancel stale requests, so a slow response for an old email can mark a new email invalid. Alternative: validate email-taken only on blur with a plain call; rejected because it still races and doesn't gate submit on pending. Chosen path: a cross-field confirm validator on the FormGroup (UI), and a proper AsyncValidatorFn for email that uses switchMap to cancel stale requests and returns ValidationErrors so Angular's PENDING/INVALID states gate the submit button correctly (async/UI); the server keeps the unique constraint authoritative and maps 409 to the email field (data). Tests cover group validity, stale cancellation, pending-disables-submit.

after_state.description: Cross-field confirm group validator + cancelable async email validator integrated with Angular validity; server-authoritative uniqueness.

files.purpose: Cross-field group validator so mismatch is a live form error
Debounced, switchMap-canceled async validator returning Angular ValidationErrors
Group validator + async validator wired so submit respects PENDING/INVALID; 409 maps to email
Authoritative unique-constraint enforcement mapped to the email field

migration_strategy: Add /api/email-available endpoint (additive) and the 409->{field:'email'} mapping on signup
Add the passwordsMatch group validator and surface the error inline
Wire emailTakenValidator with switchMap cancellation; submit gated on pending
Canary the new form; verify submit can't fire mid-check and stale checks don't mismark
Ramp to 100%; remove the submit-time alert and generic toast path
Rollback: revert validators (form falls back to submit-time checks); server changes are safe to keep
testing_strategy: Unit: passwordsMatch returns {passwordsMismatch:true} when fields differ, null when equal. Async: with a mocked HttpClient that delays, set email to A then B quickly; assert only B's result applies (switchMap cancels A) and the control is INVALID/VALID per B. Submit gating: set the email control to PENDING; assert submit() does not POST. Integration: server signup with a duplicate email returns 409 and the component sets emailTaken on the email control (side-effect: no toast). Chaos: email-available returns 500; assert catchError yields null (advisory) and the form remains usable, with the server 409 as the real guard.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A manual valueChanges subscription lives outside Angular's validity model, so the submit button isn't tied to a PENDING state and a user can submit while the email check is still in flight; and without switchMap, a slow response for a previously-typed email lands after a newer one and marks a perfectly available email as taken.

title: Refactor a Go HTTP coupon-stacking checkout form to rule-engine validation with async eligibility
business_context: An e-commerce backend (Go, chi router) validates coupon stacking with a tangle of nested ifs that's been patched for years; promotions team can't reason about which coupons stack, and edge cases let invalid stacks through (one bug gave $2.1M in unintended discounts last Black Friday). We want a declarative rule engine for stacking, async per-coupon eligibility (member tier, first-order), and the form to validate the whole cart's coupon set as a unit.
constraints: Stacking rules declarative and auditable (which rule rejected which combo)
Per-coupon eligibility is async (member tier, order history; p95 250ms) and runs in parallel
The coupon SET is validated together (some coupons are mutually exclusive)
Idempotent application: re-validating the same cart must be deterministic (no order-dependent results)
Black-Friday scale: 8k validations/sec; rule eval must be allocation-light
Backward compat: existing /checkout/validate response shape preserved (add fields, don't remove)

before_state.description: Nested-if coupon stacking with order-dependent bugs that allowed invalid discount stacks.

issues: Order-dependent: some orderings let two percent coupons stack ($2.1M bug)
No per-coupon eligibility (member tier/first-order) check
Not auditable: can't say which rule rejected
O(n^2) nested scan, allocation-heavy at scale

reasoning: Naive approach: add more nested ifs to close the specific Black-Friday hole. Rejected: the bug class is order-dependence in imperative scanning; patching one ordering leaves others, and it stays unauditable. Alternative: move stacking to the database as constraints; rejected because eligibility depends on async member/order data not in the coupon table, and DB constraints can't express 'at most one percent coupon'. Chosen path: a declarative rule set evaluated over the whole coupon SET in a deterministic, order-independent way (data/logic) producing structured violations with the rule id that fired (auditable); per-coupon eligibility runs in parallel via errgroup before set-rules (async); the response keeps the old shape plus a violations array (backward compat). Allocation-light evaluation for 8k/s. Tests cover order-independence, the $2.1M combo, parallel eligibility, determinism.

after_state.description: Declarative order-independent set-rules + parallel async eligibility + auditable violations; response back-compatible.

files.purpose: Declarative, order-independent, auditable stacking rules over the coupon set
Parallel per-coupon eligibility with collected per-code reasons
Orchestrates eligibility + set-rules into a back-compatible response with audit trail

migration_strategy: Add the rule engine + eligibility behind a shadow flag: run alongside the old ValidateCoupons and log disagreements
Investigate disagreements (the old code's order-dependent passes are the bugs); confirm rule engine is correct
Switch the response to include Violations (additive) while keeping Valid for old clients
Flip the authoritative validation from old to new behind flag coupon_rule_engine
Delete the nested-if ValidateCoupons after a clean Black-Friday dry run
Rollback: flag back to old validator (now known-buggy but available) if rule engine misbehaves
testing_strategy: Unit: AtMostOneOfType('percent').Check across all permutations of a 3-coupon set with two percents; assert it ALWAYS reports a violation (order-independence). Regression: feed the exact $2.1M Black-Friday combo; assert Valid=false with the at_most_one_percent rule id. Eligibility: mock EligSvc to fail one coupon; assert that code appears in Violations with reason and others pass, run in parallel (assert max concurrency). Determinism: run ValidateCoupons twice on the same input; assert identical Violations sets. Chaos: EligSvc times out; assert the call returns an error (fail-closed for validation) rather than silently passing an ineligible stack.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Adding more nested ifs treats a symptom; the root cause is that imperative iteration makes acceptance depend on coupon ordering, so a different permutation re-opens a stacking hole — the structural fix is evaluating the coupon set as an order-independent whole, which the nested-if approach fundamentally can't guarantee.

title: Add optimistic tag-editing with async validation and rollback to a devtools issue-tracker form
business_context: A devtools issue tracker lets users add/remove labels on an issue inline. Each label change waits for a server round trip that also runs an async automation (e.g., 'security' label notifies a team), so the chip UI lags 400-900ms and feels broken. We want optimistic chip add/remove with rollback if the label is invalid (e.g., archived label, permission denied), preserving the user's intent and never losing concurrent changes from teammates.
constraints: Adding/removing a label updates the chip UI instantly; rollback on server rejection
Some labels trigger async automations server-side; UI must not wait for automation completion
Concurrent teammate label changes (via WebSocket) must merge without clobbering the user's optimistic change
Permission/archived-label rejections roll back only that chip with a reason
Operations must be idempotent (double-add of a label is a no-op, not an error)
Label set is server-authoritative; client optimistic state reconciles to it

before_state.description: Blocking label changes wait on the server + automation; chips lag; concurrent edits clobber.

issues: Chip UI lags 400-900ms behind clicks (waits on automation)
No optimistic update or rollback
Server response replaces whole label set -> clobbers teammate changes
Double-add returns an error instead of being a no-op

reasoning: Naive approach: optimistically add the chip and replace the whole label set with the server response. Rejected: replacing the whole set discards a teammate's concurrent change that arrived between the optimistic add and the response, and waiting for the response still couples to the slow automation. Alternative: fire-and-forget the add and never reconcile; rejected because invalid labels (archived/permission) would stay shown forever. Chosen path: an op-based optimistic store — each add/remove is an idempotent operation applied locally immediately and sent async; the server applies the op (decoupling automations to a background job) and returns just the op result, not the whole set, so concurrent WS-delivered ops merge cleanly (UI/data/async); a rejected op rolls back just that chip with a reason; double-add is a server no-op. Tests cover rollback, concurrent merge, idempotency.

after_state.description: Op-based optimistic chips with per-op rollback, async-decoupled automations, WS merge, idempotent ops.

files.purpose: Op-based optimistic state: local apply, confirm/rollback per op, remote merge
Per-op idempotent API returning only the op result (not the whole set)
Authoritative idempotent op application; automations enqueued, not awaited; broadcasts op
Instant optimistic chips; per-op rollback with reason; no waiting on automation

migration_strategy: Add the op endpoint (/labels/ops) with idempotency + WS op broadcast; keep the whole-set POST for now
Move label automations (e.g., security notify) to a background job queue (decouple from the request)
Ship the optimistic op-based UI behind flag optimistic_labels off
Canary; verify chip latency drops and teammate concurrent edits merge (no clobber)
Ramp to 100%; remove the whole-set POST and blocking UI
Rollback: flag off restores blocking labels; op endpoint stays inert
testing_strategy: Store test: applyLocal add, then rollback; assert the label is removed and pending cleared. Concurrent merge: applyLocal a local add, then mergeRemote a teammate's add of a different label; assert both labels present (no clobber). Rejection: mock sendOp to 403; assert the optimistically-added chip is rolled back and a reason toast shown. Idempotency: send the same op id twice to applyOp; assert a single DB mutation and one opLog entry. Chaos: server enqueues the security automation but the job worker is down; assert the label still applies immediately (automation decoupled) and the chip is not blocked.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Replacing the whole label set with the server response overwrites a teammate's label that was added between your optimistic change and the response — a lost update visible as a flickering, reverting chip; and coupling the request to the security-notify automation keeps the 400-900ms lag that made the chips feel broken in the first place.

title: Add schema-driven validation and async tax-ID verification to a B2B vendor-onboarding form (FastAPI + Vue)
business_context: A B2B procurement platform onboards vendors with a 12-field form (legal name, tax ID, bank, addresses). Validation is split between a Vue component and a FastAPI endpoint that disagree, and tax-ID verification (against a government registry, p95 1.5s) only happens at final submit, so 13% of vendors complete everything before a tax-ID rejection. We want one Pydantic-derived schema shared with the client and async tax-ID verification surfaced inline with graceful degradation.
constraints: One schema (Pydantic) is the source of truth; client validation derived from it (JSON Schema export)
Tax-ID verification is async (p95 1.5s, sometimes 8s); must time out at 3s and degrade to 'pending verification'
Country-conditional fields (EU VAT vs US EIN) via conditional schema
Verification calls are rate-limited and cached (same tax ID not re-verified within 24h)
PII (bank, tax ID) encrypted at rest; never logged
Backward compat: in-flight onboarding drafts (old shape) still load

before_state.description: Divergent client/server validation; tax-ID verified only at submit; late rejection; no caching.

issues: Tax ID verified only at submit -> 13% late rejection
Client (Vue) validation diverges from this server logic
No timeout: an 8s registry call stalls submit
No caching -> re-verifies same tax id repeatedly

reasoning: Naive approach: copy the FastAPI checks into the Vue component. Rejected: two hand-maintained validators drift on the next field change, and it doesn't address late tax-ID verification or the 8s stall. Alternative: verify tax ID client-side by calling the registry directly; rejected because the registry requires server credentials and rate limits we can't expose. Chosen path: define one Pydantic model with country-conditional validators (data/source of truth) and export its JSON Schema to the client so the Vue form validates from the SAME definition; a debounced async tax-ID verify endpoint with a 3s timeout that degrades to 'pending' and caches results 24h (async/perf); PII fields encrypted at rest (config). Tests cover schema parity, timeout degradation, cache, and conditional fields; rollout keeps old draft shapes loadable.

after_state.description: Single Pydantic schema exported to client; async tax-ID verify with timeout/degrade/cache; conditional fields; encrypted PII.

files.purpose: Single source-of-truth schema with country-conditional rules; exported to client

files.content:
import asyncio
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
_cache: dict[str, tuple[datetime, str]] = {}
async def verify_tax_id(tax_id: str) -> str:
    hit=_cache.get(tax_id)
    if hit and datetime.utcnow()-hit[0] < timedelta(hours=24):
        return hit[1]
    try:
        ok = await asyncio.wait_for(registry.verify(tax_id), timeout=3.0)
        state = 'verified' if ok else 'invalid'
    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
        return 'pending'  # degrade; do not cache a pending
    _cache[tax_id]=(datetime.utcnow(), state)
    return state
files.purpose: Async verify with 3s timeout, 24h cache, and 'pending' degradation
Authoritative schema validation + verify with pending allowance + encrypted bank
Client validates against the exported Pydantic JSON Schema + async tax-ID feedback

migration_strategy: Define the Pydantic Vendor model; make the server use it (authoritative); export JSON Schema artifact
Add the /vendors/verify-tax endpoint with timeout + 24h cache (no UI change yet)
Wire the Vue form to validate from the generated schema + show async tax state behind flag vendor_schema_form
Allow 'pending' tax_state on create with a background re-verify job; canary a region
Ramp; retire the divergent Vue validation code
Rollback: flag off reverts client UI; server schema + verify (pending-tolerant) safe to keep
testing_strategy: Unit: Vendor(country='DE', vat_id=None) raises 'vat_id required'; US without 9-digit EIN raises. Parity: validate a set of fixtures with both Ajv (client schema) and Pydantic (server); assert identical accept/reject. Timeout: monkeypatch registry.verify to sleep 8s; assert verify_tax_id returns 'pending' within ~3s and does NOT cache pending. Cache: verify the same tax_id twice; assert registry.verify called once. Integration: create_vendor with a pending tax state; assert the row is created with tax_state='pending' and bank stored encrypted (negative: raw bank string absent from the column). Chaos: registry down; assert create still succeeds with 'pending' and a re-verify job enqueued.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Copying the FastAPI checks into Vue produces two validators that drift the next time a country rule changes, re-introducing client/server disagreement; and a blocking, no-timeout registry call means the registry's occasional 8s latency stalls every submit, so the late-rejection problem becomes a hang on top of it.

title: Build a reusable async-validation engine with request coalescing for a high-traffic social-handle picker
business_context: A social network's profile-edit form checks handle availability, display-name profanity, and bio-link safety — three independent async validators currently implemented ad hoc per field with copy-pasted debounce/abort logic that's subtly different and buggy (one leaks aborted-request state). At 50M MAU, profile edits are frequent and the validators hit three different services. We want one async-validation engine that standardizes debounce, abort, dedupe, and result-staleness across all three.
constraints: One engine handles N async field validators with consistent debounce/abort/dedupe semantics
Stale results (field changed again) must never overwrite newer state — per-field request generation tokens
Identical in-flight requests for the same value must coalesce (dedupe) across components
Each validator service has its own rate budget; engine must respect per-validator concurrency caps
Validators can fail-open (handle check: fail-closed; profanity: fail-open) configurable per field
Server re-runs all three as the authoritative gate on save

before_state.description: Three copy-pasted async validators with divergent, partly-buggy debounce/abort logic.

issues: No AbortController -> stale response overwrites newer state
Debounce/abort copy-pasted differently in 3 places
No dedupe across components hitting the same service
No per-validator concurrency cap or fail-open policy

reasoning: Naive approach: fix the abort bug in each of the three copies. Rejected: three divergent copies will drift again and each needs the same dedupe/concurrency/fail-open features re-implemented; the bug class (stale overwrite) recurs. Alternative: adopt a heavy form library's async validation; rejected because it doesn't give per-validator concurrency caps or cross-component dedupe at our scale and couples us to its form model. Chosen path: one AsyncValidationEngine that, per field, tracks a generation token so only the latest request's result applies (staleness), an in-flight map keyed by (field,value) for cross-component dedupe, a per-validator semaphore for concurrency budget, and a fail-open/closed policy per field (UI/infra). The server re-runs all three as the authoritative save gate (data). Tests cover staleness, dedupe, concurrency cap, fail policies.

after_state.description: Single async-validation engine with generation tokens, dedupe, per-validator semaphores, configurable fail policy; authoritative server gate.

files.purpose: Unified engine: generation-token staleness, dedupe, per-validator concurrency, fail policy
Declares the three validators with per-field concurrency + fail policy
Thin hook: debounce + engine; ignores stale results
Authoritative server re-run of all three validators as the save gate

migration_strategy: Introduce the engine + validators config; migrate the handle field to it first (highest traffic)
Verify staleness/dedupe behavior in canary; compare error rates vs the old copy-pasted checker
Migrate displayName and bioLink fields to the engine, deleting their bespoke debounce/abort code
Tune per-validator concurrency caps from observed service load
Make server saveProfile the authoritative gate for all three (already additive)
Rollback: revert a field to its old checker individually (engine is per-field) if needed
testing_strategy: Engine unit: call validate('handle','a') then validate('handle','ab') with the first resolving last; assert the first result is marked stale and ignored. Dedupe: two concurrent validate('handle','foo'); assert the underlying fetch fires once. Concurrency: fire 20 validate calls on a concurrency-8 field; assert no more than 8 run simultaneously (spy on inflight). Fail policy: make the profanity service throw; assert displayName fails OPEN (ok:true) while a thrown handle check fails CLOSED (ok:false). Integration: saveProfile with a taken handle returns 422 errors.handle and (side-effect) no profile update.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Fixing the abort bug in each of the three copies leaves three implementations that re-diverge on the next change and each still lacks cross-component dedupe and per-validator concurrency caps; at 50M MAU the missing dedupe means identical in-flight handle checks multiply load on the handle service, and the recurring stale-overwrite bug keeps flashing wrong availability to users.

title: Add async warehouse-capacity validation with optimistic reservation to a logistics shipment form (NestJS + Redis)
business_context: A 3PL platform lets merchants schedule inbound shipments to warehouses with limited daily receiving slots. The form lets merchants pick a date+warehouse and only learns capacity is full at submit, after they've entered carton counts and carrier info. ~8% of bookings bounce, and ops manually reschedules. We want live capacity validation that reserves capacity optimistically as the merchant configures the shipment.
constraints: Daily receiving capacity per warehouse is finite (e.g., 500 pallets/day); concurrent merchants compete
Selecting a date+warehouse must soft-reserve the pallet count optimistically; release on abandon (TTL 10m)
Capacity is per (warehouse, date) and must be correct across multiple API instances (Redis)
Changing pallet count must adjust the reservation atomically (no negative capacity)
Final confirm converts soft reservation to a firm booking atomically
Backward compat: existing capacity-report endpoint stays unchanged

before_state.description: Capacity checked only at submit; concurrent merchants oversubscribe; late bounce.

issues: Read-then-write race: two merchants both pass, oversubscribe
Capacity only known at submit -> 8% late bounce
No optimistic reservation as the form is built
Per-instance reads can be stale

reasoning: Naive approach: wrap the read+write in a DB transaction with SELECT...FOR UPDATE on a capacity row. Rejected: it fixes the oversubscribe race but the merchant still only learns capacity is gone at submit after configuring everything, and it serializes all bookings for a warehouse/day through one row lock under contention. Alternative: cache remaining capacity in Redis read-only for display; rejected because a display-only number still lets two merchants race past it. Chosen path: maintain remaining capacity as a Redis counter per (warehouse,date); selecting/adjusting pallets does an atomic DECRBY with a Lua guard that refuses to go negative and creates a soft reservation with a 10m TTL keyed to the merchant session (data/async); confirm converts the soft reservation into a DB booking atomically and persists the consumed capacity; abandon/TTL returns the pallets via INCRBY (config). The capacity-report endpoint is unchanged. Tests cover atomic decrement, no-negative, TTL release, confirm.

after_state.description: Redis capacity counters with atomic optimistic reservation, no-negative guard, TTL release, atomic confirm.

files.purpose: Atomic reserve (no-negative) + TTL hold + release returning capacity
Reserve/adjust/confirm flow turning optimistic holds into firm bookings
Optimistic reserve on select + release on abandon; shows live remaining capacity

migration_strategy: Backfill Redis capacity counters from current DB usage per (warehouse,date)
Add reserve/adjust/confirm/release endpoints; keep the old book() path live
Ship the optimistic form behind flag shipment_reserve off
Canary one busy warehouse; verify no oversubscription and abandons release within TTL
Ramp; switch confirm to the authoritative booking path; deprecate old book()
Rollback: flag off restores book() (now backed by a FOR UPDATE capacity row as a safety net)
testing_strategy: Unit: reserve with pallets > remaining returns ok:false and does NOT decrement (assert counter unchanged). Concurrency: two reserve calls for the last 100 pallets each on a 150-remaining day; assert exactly one succeeds and the counter never goes negative. TTL: set hold TTL to 1s in test; after expiry, assert release/incrby logic (or a sweeper) returns capacity. Confirm: confirm with an expired hold returns 409 and creates no booking. Chaos: Redis flush mid-flow; assert confirm fails closed (no overbooking) and merchant is prompted to retry, capacity reconciled from DB.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A FOR UPDATE capacity row prevents oversubscription but still only reveals 'full' at submit after the merchant configured the whole shipment (the 8% bounce remains), and it funnels every booking for a hot warehouse/day through a single row lock, serializing peak intake; a display-only Redis number, conversely, lets two merchants race past the same shown remaining count.

title: Migrate a gaming clan-settings form to schema-driven validation with async name-moderation and rollback
business_context: A mobile game's clan-settings form (React Native + Node) lets leaders set clan name, tag, and description. Name moderation (async AI + banned-word list, p95 700ms) runs only at save, and the form blocks for ~1s while saving; bad names slip through when the AI service is slow and is skipped. With 200k clans, name disputes generate heavy support load. We want schema-driven validation, optimistic save with rollback on moderation rejection, and a deterministic banned-word pre-check.
constraints: Banned-word pre-check (deterministic, client+server) runs instantly; AI moderation runs async
Optimistic save shows the new name immediately; rollback to old name if moderation rejects
AI moderation timeout (1.2s) must fail to 'pending review' (name shown but flagged), never silently allow
Concurrent leaders editing the same clan must not clobber (version check)
Profanity/banned list is shared by client and server (single source)
Backward compat: clan settings API response shape preserved

before_state.description: Moderation only at save; UI blocks ~1s; AI skip on slowness lets bad names through; no version check.

issues: Moderation only at save -> UI blocks ~1s
AI skipped when slow -> bad names slip through
No optimistic UI / rollback
No version check -> concurrent leaders clobber

reasoning: Naive approach: optimistically set the name and revert on a non-OK response. Rejected: it doesn't distinguish a banned-word reject (deterministic, instant) from an AI timeout (should become 'pending review', not a hard reject or silent allow), and offers no concurrency guard so two leaders clobber. Alternative: keep blocking save but add a spinner; rejected because it doesn't fix the 1s freeze or the AI-skip-on-slowness loophole. Chosen path: a shared banned-word list + schema validate name format/length instantly client+server (data/shared); optimistic save snapshots the old name, applies the new one, and sends a PUT with the clan version (UI/data); server runs the deterministic banlist (hard reject) then AI moderation with a 1.2s timeout that yields status 'pending' (name kept, flagged) — never a silent allow (async); version mismatch -> 409 rollback. Tests cover banlist, timeout->pending, rollback, version conflict.

after_state.description: Shared banlist + schema, optimistic versioned save, deterministic reject vs AI timeout->pending, rollback.

files.purpose: Shared deterministic format + banned-word rules (client and server)
Optimistic versioned save with rollback and distinct reject/pending/conflict states
Authoritative banlist + AI moderation with timeout->pending, version guard

migration_strategy: Extract the banned-word list + nameSchema; server uses it as the deterministic pre-check
Add If-Match version handling to PUT /api/clans/:id (backward-compatible if header absent)
Add AI moderation timeout->pending semantics with a background re-moderation job for pending names
Ship the optimistic UI behind flag clan_optimistic_name off
Canary; verify rollback on reject and that slow AI yields 'pending' (flagged), not silent allow
Rollback: flag off restores blocking save; server banlist + timeout semantics stay
testing_strategy: Unit: nameSchema rejects a name containing a banned substring and a 2-char name. Component: mock PUT returning moderation:'rejected'; assert the name reverts to the prior value and status='rejected'. Timeout: make ai.moderate hang; assert updateClan returns moderation:'pending' within ~1.2s and (side-effect) the clan row is saved with moderation='pending' and a re-moderation job enqueued. Concurrency: two saves with the same If-Match; assert the second gets 409 and the UI rolls back. Chaos: AI service errors (not timeout); assert it is treated as 'pending' (flagged), never a silent approve.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Reverting only on a non-OK response collapses three different outcomes — a banned-word reject, an AI rejection, and an AI timeout — into one, so a slow moderation service either blocks the user or (in the legacy skip-on-slow behavior) silently lets a bad clan name through; and with no version check, two co-leaders editing settings clobber each other's changes.

title: Add conditional required-field logic and async dependent-dropdown validation to a Django insurance quote form
business_context: An insurance broker's quote form (Django + HTMX) has a vehicle section where make->model->trim are dependent dropdowns, but the form lets users submit a model that doesn't belong to the selected make (data entered out of order), producing invalid quotes that underwriters reject (~12% rework). We want async dependent-dropdown population and validation that the chosen model/trim actually belongs to the make, enforced server-side too.
constraints: Model options load async based on make; trim options async based on model
Server must validate the (make,model,trim) tuple is internally consistent (advisory client check)
Changing make must reset model+trim (no stale invalid combo persists)
Async option fetches debounced/cached; reference data is large (50k models)
Conditional required: 'prior claims detail' required only if has_prior_claims=yes
Works without JS (server re-renders dependent options) — progressive enhancement

issues: No (make,model,trim) consistency check -> invalid quotes (12% rework)
Changing make doesn't reset model/trim
Conditional 'prior claims detail' not enforced
Dependent options not loaded async

reasoning: Naive approach: validate model-belongs-to-make only in JavaScript on change. Rejected: out-of-order data entry and JS-disabled clients still submit mismatched tuples, and underwriters still get bad quotes — the rework persists. Alternative: a single giant select of all 50k model strings; rejected as unusable and huge. Chosen path: HTMX-driven dependent endpoints return model options for a make and trim options for a model, cached (UI/async); selecting a make resets model+trim server-side and client-side; the submit view authoritatively validates the (make,model,trim) tuple against reference data and enforces the conditional 'prior claims detail' requirement (data); progressive enhancement means the dependent selects re-render server-side without JS. Tests cover tuple consistency, make-reset, conditional requirement, no-JS path.

after_state.description: HTMX dependent dropdowns with cached options, authoritative tuple + conditional validation, make-reset, no-JS support.

Dependent option endpoints + authoritative tuple & conditional validation
HTMX dependent selects; changing make swaps and resets model+trim

Add authoritative tuple_valid + conditional check to quote() in log-only mode (record would-be rejections)
Once log-only confirms the rework population, enforce 422 on invalid tuples
Convert the vehicle section to HTMX dependent selects (make-change resets model+trim)
Measure underwriter rework rate drop; tune cache sizes
Rollback: enforcement back to log-only; HTMX selects degrade to plain selects with server validation
testing_strategy: Unit: tuple_valid('Toyota','Civic','') is False (Civic is Honda); tuple_valid('Honda','Civic','EX') is True. View: POST a mismatched make/model; assert 422 with errors['model'] and (side-effect) no Quote row. Conditional: POST has_prior_claims=yes with empty detail; assert 422 errors['prior_claims_detail']. No-JS: GET /quotes/models?make=Honda renders Honda models (server-side dependency). Make-reset: simulate changing make; assert the model+trim region is replaced (no stale combo). Chaos: refdata cache cold + DB slow; assert the endpoint still returns within the request budget (lru_cache warms) and validation never passes an invalid tuple.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Validating model-belongs-to-make only in JavaScript leaves the out-of-order-entry and JS-disabled paths wide open, so mismatched (make,model,trim) tuples keep reaching underwriters and the 12% rework continues; the consistency rule has to be authoritative on the server, where the naive approach never puts it.

title: Add optimistic quantity edits with debounced server reconciliation to a B2B cart form (Next.js + tRPC)
business_context: A B2B wholesale ordering app (Next.js + tRPC) has a cart where buyers adjust line-item quantities. Each +/- click fires a server call that recomputes tier pricing, taxes, and freight (p95 600ms), so rapid quantity changes queue up and the totals flicker through intermediate states. Buyers ordering 80+ SKUs find it sluggish and occasionally see wrong totals when responses arrive out of order. We want optimistic quantity edits with debounced, order-safe server reconciliation of pricing.
constraints: Quantity changes update the line + subtotal optimistically using client-side tier pricing rules
Server recompute (taxes/freight/tier verification) debounced per line and coalesced
Out-of-order server responses must never overwrite a newer optimistic state (sequence guard)
Min/max order quantity and pack-size multiples validated client + server
Concurrent edits across 80 lines must each reconcile independently
Server price is authoritative; optimistic price reconciles to it at checkout

before_state.description: Per-click server recompute; totals flicker; out-of-order responses show wrong totals.

issues: Every click hits the server (600ms) -> sluggish at 80 SKUs
Totals flicker through intermediate states
Out-of-order responses overwrite newer state
Pack-size multiples not validated

reasoning: Naive approach: debounce the server call per line. Rejected: debouncing alone still lets a late response from an earlier debounce window overwrite a newer optimistic quantity if the user keeps clicking, and it doesn't validate pack-size multiples instantly. Alternative: keep everything server-side but batch all 80 lines into one recompute; rejected because a single click on one line shouldn't recompute the entire cart and still leaves the latency for that line. Chosen path: client applies the quantity change and a client-side tier-price estimate optimistically (UI), validating pack-size/min/max instantly via a shared rules module (data/shared); a per-line debounced tRPC recompute carries a monotonically increasing seq so the reducer ignores any response with a stale seq (async/order-safety); server is authoritative for taxes/freight and final tier verification, reconciled into the line. Tests cover seq guard, pack-size, reconciliation, independent lines.

after_state.description: Optimistic qty + client tier estimate, per-line debounced recompute with sequence guard, shared pack-size rules, authoritative reconciliation.

files.purpose: Shared pack-size normalization + client-side tier price estimate (client & server)
Optimistic per-line state with monotonic seq; reconcile ignores stale responses
Per-line debounced recompute that passes the seq through to the reconciler
Authoritative server recompute (tier+tax+freight) with pack-size enforcement

migration_strategy: Add the shared qtyRules + tRPC recompute procedure (authoritative); keep updateQty for now
Introduce the optimistic cartStore with seq guard behind flag optimistic_cart off
Canary a few buyers; verify totals stop flickering and out-of-order responses don't stick
Add per-line debounced recompute; validate pack-size snapping matches server
Ramp to all; deprecate the per-click updateQty
Rollback: flag off restores per-click server recompute; recompute procedure stays
testing_strategy: Store unit: setQty twice quickly then reconcile with the FIRST seq; assert the stale reconcile is ignored (seq mismatch) and the line keeps the newer qty. Pack-size: setQty(7) on a packSize-5 SKU snaps to 5; server recompute also snaps to 5 (parity). Independence: edit line A and line B; assert each reconciles with its own seq without affecting the other. Integration: recompute returns base+tax+freight; assert the line total equals the authoritative sum (side-effect: db.cartLine.qty updated). Chaos: server recompute for an old qty resolves after a new edit; assert reconcile drops it (seq guard) so the displayed total matches the latest qty.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Per-line debouncing without a sequence guard still allows a late response from an earlier debounce window to land after the user has clicked again, so a buyer who quickly bumps 10->12->15 can see the total snap back to the price for 12 — exactly the out-of-order wrong-total bug; debounce reduces calls but doesn't make responses order-safe.

title: Introduce a typed schema registry for cross-service form validation in a microservices fintech onboarding flow
business_context: A fintech onboarding spans 4 microservices (identity, KYC, banking, compliance), each with its own copy of the applicant schema. They've drifted: the web form sends a field the banking service silently drops, and compliance rejects records the web form considers valid. ~6% of applications stall in limbo between services. We want a single versioned schema registry that every service and the web form validate against, with controlled schema evolution.
constraints: One canonical applicant schema, versioned, consumed by web form + 4 services
Schema evolution must be backward compatible (additive) or gated by an explicit major version
Each service validates inbound payloads against the pinned schema version (contract enforcement)
Web form validation derived from the same registry artifact (no hand copies)
A schema change must be CI-gated: no breaking change merges without a major bump
In-flight applications keep validating against the version they started with

before_state.description: Four divergent copies of the applicant schema; silent field drops; cross-service rejections; stalled apps.

issues: 4 copies drifted; banking drops fields web sends
Compliance requires a field banking's schema doesn't know
No version pinning -> ambiguous which schema applies
No CI gate on breaking changes

reasoning: Naive approach: copy compliance's schema into the other three services to re-sync. Rejected: a one-time manual sync drifts again on the next change and there's still no version pinning or CI gate, so the limbo recurs. Alternative: validate only at the edge (web/API gateway) and trust internally; rejected because internal services consume events/messages directly and a drift there silently drops fields. Chosen path: a published, versioned schema package (registry) generated once and consumed by the web form and all four services (data/source of truth); each service pins a major version and validates inbound payloads against it (contract); CI runs a compatibility check that fails the build if a change is breaking without a major bump (config/governance); in-flight applications carry their schema version so the right rules apply (data). Tests cover compatibility detection, cross-service parity, version pinning.

after_state.description: Versioned schema registry consumed everywhere, per-service version pinning, CI compatibility gate, version-stamped applications.

files.purpose: Single canonical, versioned applicant schema consumed by all services + web
Shared validator + a major-version pin assertion for each service
Banking validates inbound against the registry; no field silently dropped
CI gate: fails the build if a schema change is breaking without a major version bump

migration_strategy: Publish the canonical schema package at the union of all services' current fields (superset)
Switch each service to import and validate against the package, one service per PR (parity verified)
Wire the web form to the same package artifact, deleting its local copy
Add the CI compatibility test gating breaking changes on a major bump
Stamp applications with APPLICANT_SCHEMA_VERSION; in-flight apps pin their version
Rollback: a service can temporarily re-pin the previous schema package version if a change misbehaves
testing_strategy: Unit: validateApplicant rejects a payload missing taxResidency (the field that caused drift) and one with additionalProperties. Parity: feed 1k fixtures through the banking, KYC, and compliance validators; assert identical accept/reject (all import the same compiled schema). CI gate: add a new required field in a test fixture schema without a major bump; assert compat.test fails. Version pinning: a payload tagged schemaVersion 2.x validates against major-2; a 3.x payload to a major-2 service triggers assertVersion error. Chaos: a service receives a field not in the schema (additionalProperties:false); assert it rejects with a contract error rather than silently dropping it.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A one-time manual copy of compliance's schema into the other services re-drifts on the very next field change because nothing keeps them in lockstep and nothing pins versions, so the same cross-service limbo (banking silently dropping a field compliance requires) reappears — only a shared versioned artifact plus a CI compatibility gate structurally prevents the drift.

title: Add async seat-availability validation with hold to an event-ticketing seat-selection form (Go + Postgres)
business_context: An event-ticketing platform (Go backend, React front) lets buyers pick specific seats from a map. The form lets two buyers select the same seat and both proceed to payment, with the loser failing at the payment step after entering card details. During on-sales (10k concurrent), this causes a surge of failed payments and chargebacks. We want seats held the moment they're selected, with the hold released on timeout, and a final atomic purchase.
constraints: Selecting a seat must place a hold visible to all other buyers immediately
Holds expire after 7 minutes; expiry frees the seat for others
10k concurrent buyers during on-sale; seat-hold writes must not deadlock
Purchase converts holds to sold atomically; partial purchases (some seats sold) not allowed
Hold ownership tied to a session; only the holder can purchase the held seats
Seat map UI must reflect held/sold states in near-real-time

before_state.description: Seats selectable freely; double-selection; payment-time failure for the loser; chargebacks.

issues: No hold on selection -> two buyers pick same seat
Check only at purchase -> failure after card entry
IsSold then MarkSold is a race (TOCTOU)
No partial-purchase atomicity

reasoning: Naive approach: at purchase, lock each seat row FOR UPDATE and mark sold. Rejected: prevents the double-sell but the collision is still discovered at payment after card entry (chargeback-inducing), and locking many seat rows in inconsistent order across 10k concurrent buyers risks deadlocks. Alternative: hold seats in Redis only; rejected because the sale-of-record must be in Postgres for accounting/atomicity and a Redis-only hold can desync from the sold table. Chosen path: a seat_holds table with a unique (event_id, seat_id) partial index where status='held'/'sold' so an INSERT...ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING is an atomic claim-or-fail at selection time (data); holds carry an expires_at and a session owner; a sweeper (or lazy check) frees expired holds; purchase converts the buyer's held rows to sold in one transaction, ordered by seat_id to avoid deadlocks, all-or-nothing (data/concurrency); the seat map subscribes to hold/sold changes (UI). Tests cover atomic claim, expiry, atomic purchase, deadlock-free ordering.

after_state.description: Atomic seat holds via unique-index INSERT-on-conflict, TTL expiry, deadlock-safe atomic purchase, real-time map.

files.purpose: Atomic seat claim on selection via unique-index ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
Atomic, deadlock-safe hold->sold conversion; all-or-nothing
Periodic sweeper frees expired holds and notifies the seat map
Real-time seat states + optimistic select reflecting hold conflicts

migration_strategy: Add seat_holds table + unique partial index; backfill sold seats as status='sold'
Add HoldSeat endpoint and the sweeper; the seat map starts reflecting holds
Switch Purchase to the atomic hold->sold conversion behind flag seat_holds off (legacy default)
Canary a small on-sale; verify no double-sell and expired holds free up
Ramp to major on-sales; remove the legacy IsSold/MarkSold path
Rollback: flag off restores legacy purchase (now backed by FOR UPDATE) if holds misbehave
testing_strategy: Concurrency: two HoldSeat calls for the same seat in parallel; assert exactly one returns true (side-effect: one seat_holds row). Expiry: insert a hold with expires_at in the past; run SweepExpired; assert the row is gone and the seat is holdable again. Purchase atomicity: purchase 3 seats where one hold expired; assert the whole transaction rolls back (no seat marked sold). Deadlock: run 100 concurrent purchases over overlapping seat sets; assert no deadlock errors (sorted ordering). Chaos: kill the sweeper; assert purchase still rejects expired holds (checked in-tx), so correctness doesn't depend on the sweeper.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A purchase-time FOR UPDATE stops the double-sell at the database but the buyer who loses still discovers it after entering card details (the chargeback driver), and locking many seat rows in arbitrary order across 10k concurrent purchases invites deadlocks; the fix has to claim the seat atomically at selection time, which the naive purchase-only locking never does.

title: Refactor a Vue/Nuxt survey-builder to schema-driven conditional logic with async branching validation
business_context: A survey SaaS lets creators build forms with branching ('skip to Q7 if answer is No'). The runtime form (Nuxt) hardcodes branching in a 900-line component, so creators can't add branches without an engineer, and ~7% of published surveys have broken skip-logic (asks questions that should be skipped, then validates them as required). We want a declarative branching schema interpreted at runtime, with required-validation respecting active branches.
constraints: Branching defined declaratively (when answer X, show/skip questions Y) and stored per survey version
Required validation must only apply to questions on the active path (skipped questions aren't required)
Branching can depend on multiple prior answers (AND/OR conditions)
Server validates submissions against the same active-path logic (client advisory)
Large surveys (200 questions) must compute the active path in <30ms per answer change
Backward compat: existing published surveys keep rendering during migration

before_state.description: Hardcoded branching in a giant component; skip-logic bugs; skipped questions wrongly required.

files.content:
<script setup>
const answers=ref({});
function visible(qid){
  if(qid==='q7' && answers.value.q3==='No') return false; // hardcoded per survey
  // 900 lines of bespoke branch conditions
  return true;
}
function validate(){ return survey.questions.filter(q=>q.required).every(q=>answers.value[q.id]); } // ignores visibility -> requires skipped
</script>
issues: Branching hardcoded -> creators can't edit
validate() requires skipped questions (ignores visibility)
7% of surveys have broken skip-logic
No server-side path validation

reasoning: Naive approach: extract the bespoke conditions into a config object the component reads. Rejected: a flat config still needs the component to interpret multi-answer AND/OR conditions and the required-validation still ignores visibility unless rewritten. Alternative: precompute every path server-side as a decision tree; rejected because answers are dynamic and the tree for 200 questions with multi-condition branches explodes. Chosen path: a declarative rule format (conditions reference prior answers with AND/OR) stored per survey version (data); a runtime engine computes the active set of visible questions from current answers in one pass (UI/perf); required-validation runs ONLY over visible questions (the core bug fix); the server runs the identical engine on submit (shared). Tests cover branch visibility, skipped-not-required, multi-condition, perf, server parity; rollout per-survey-version.

after_state.description: Declarative branching engine; required-validation scoped to the active path; shared server validation; fast.

files.purpose: Declarative AND/OR branching engine computing the active question set
Required-validation scoped to the active path (fixes the skipped-required bug)

files.content:
<script setup>
import { computed, ref } from 'vue';
import { activeQuestions } from '@/src/survey/branching';
import { validateSurvey } from '@/src/survey/validate';
const props=defineProps(['survey']);
const answers=ref({});
const visible=computed(()=>activeQuestions(props.survey.questions.map(q=>q.id), props.survey.rules, answers.value));
const errors=ref({});
function submit(){ errors.value=validateSurvey(props.survey, answers.value); if(!Object.keys(errors.value).length) emit('done', answers.value); }
</script>
<template><template v-for="q in survey.questions" :key="q.id"><Question v-if="visible.has(q.id)" :q="q" v-model="answers[q.id]" :error="errors[q.id]"/></template></template>
files.purpose: Generic runtime rendering active questions from the engine; validation respects visibility
Server runs the same branching engine for authoritative validation

migration_strategy: Build the branching engine; convert one existing survey's hardcoded conditions to declarative rules and assert identical visibility
Add a versioned rules column; server submit uses the engine in log-only mode
Render the generic SurveyRuntime for new surveys behind flag declarative_branching off
Migrate published surveys' conditions to rules one version at a time, retiring bespoke code
Enable server enforcement of active-path validation
Rollback: per-survey-version flag back to the bespoke component
testing_strategy: Unit: activeQuestions hides q7 when q3='No' and shows it otherwise; a rule with all+any conditions activates only when both groups satisfied. Core bug: validateSurvey with q7 hidden and unanswered returns NO error for q7 (skipped not required); with q7 visible and unanswered, returns a required error. Perf: 200 questions + 150 rules; assert activeQuestions runs <30ms. Parity: run validateSurvey on client and server fixtures; assert identical errors. Chaos: an answer referencing a deleted question id; assert the engine treats the condition as unmatched (no crash) and visibility stays sane.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Extracting conditions into a flat config doesn't fix the central bug: validate() still iterates required questions without consulting visibility, so a skipped question stays 'required' and blocks submission — the 7%-broken-survey symptom — unless required-validation is explicitly scoped to the engine's active set, which the naive config refactor doesn't do.

title: Add file-upload validation with async virus scan and optimistic preview to a B2B document form (Express + S3)
business_context: A B2B contract-management app lets users attach documents. The current form uploads to S3 and only validates type/size server-side after the full upload completes, so a user can wait 30s for a 50MB upload only to be told the type is wrong. There's also no virus scanning, a compliance gap. We want instant client-side type/size validation, optimistic upload preview, and async virus scanning with the document blocked until clean.
constraints: Client validates MIME type + size before upload starts (reject early, no wasted bandwidth)
Server re-validates type by magic bytes (not just extension) — clients lie
Virus scan is async (ClamAV, p95 4s); document is 'pending scan', usable only after 'clean'
Optimistic preview shows the file immediately but marks it 'scanning'
Infected files are quarantined and the user notified; no infected file reachable
Backward compat: existing documents (pre-scan) treated as 'legacy-clean'

before_state.description: Type/size validated only after full upload; no virus scan; wasted 30s uploads on wrong type.

issues: Type checked only after full 50MB upload (30s wasted)
Trusts client-provided mimetype (spoofable)
No virus scanning (compliance gap)
Whole file buffered in memory

reasoning: Naive approach: add a ClamAV scan synchronously after upload before responding. Rejected: a 4s p95 scan blocks the request and a large queue during busy periods times out uploads; type is still checked only after the full upload. Alternative: scan in a nightly batch; rejected because infected files would be reachable until the batch runs (the compliance gap remains). Chosen path: client validates MIME+size before starting the upload (UI) so wrong types never consume bandwidth; the server streams to S3 and re-validates by magic bytes (data/security) since extensions lie; the document is stored as status='pending_scan' and an async ClamAV job scans it, flipping to 'clean' or 'quarantined' (async); the UI shows an optimistic preview marked 'scanning' and only enables the doc when 'clean' (UI). Tests cover early reject, magic-byte spoof, scan-then-clean, quarantine.

after_state.description: Client pre-validation, magic-byte server check, async virus scan with pending/clean/quarantined states, optimistic preview.

files.purpose: Client MIME+size pre-validation; optimistic preview; scan polling
Magic-byte type re-validation + store as pending_scan + enqueue async scan
Async ClamAV scan; quarantine-or-clean; infected files made unreachable
Gate access: only clean/legacy-clean documents are served

migration_strategy: Add client MIME+size pre-validation (pure UX win; rejects before bandwidth use)
Add magic-byte server validation (stricter; reject spoofed types)
Add status column; mark all existing docs 'legacy-clean'; new docs 'pending_scan' with async scan
Gate /docs/:id access on status; UI shows scanning/clean/infected
Enable for a pilot tenant; verify quarantine flow and no infected file served
Rollback: disable scan enqueue (docs go straight to clean) if the scanner is unavailable, behind a flag
testing_strategy: Unit: client upload() rejects a .exe (wrong MIME) and a 60MB file before any fetch. Magic-byte: POST a file named report.pdf whose bytes are actually a ZIP; assert 415 (extension lie caught). Scan flow: enqueue scan for a clean fixture; assert status transitions pending_scan->clean and access serves a signed URL. Quarantine: feed the EICAR test string; assert status='quarantined', the original S3 key deleted, a quarantine copy exists, and /docs/:id returns 451. Chaos: scanner worker down; assert the doc stays 'pending_scan' and access returns 425 (never serves an unscanned file).
what_breaks_naive_approach: A synchronous ClamAV scan ties every upload response to a 4s scan, so during busy periods the scan queue backs up and uploads time out; and trusting the client mimetype lets a renamed executable through — only magic-byte validation plus an async, access-gated scan closes both the UX and the compliance gap without blocking the upload request.
